items: U.S. equities entered the week after a rare losing week and five consecutive S&P 500 declines.
Weekend developments around U.S.-Iran diplomacy reduced immediate tail risk but did not remove oil/geopolitical uncertainty.
AI and semiconductor shares were positioned for a rebound after sharp prior-week volatility.
scheduled_events: No single scheduled macro release dominated the session setup; the key known variables were oil, Middle East headlines and whether AI leadership could stabilize.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Broad risk rebound after five straight S&P 500 losses.
AI and chip shares rebounded strongly, helped by large announced South Korean chip-investment plans.
Oil rose but Treasury yields were comparatively stable, allowing growth stocks to recover.
causal_narrative: The session was primarily a relief and positioning rebound. The index recovered from a compressed, oversold five-day decline as AI leadership re-engaged. Geopolitical risk remained present, but stable yields prevented it from overwhelming the equity rebound.
source_notes: Washington Post/AP: U.S. stocks rise and recover losses from a rare losing week
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
